message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: My task: Add validate_nesting param to Context (core.py) enforcing child-context close tracking, RuntimeError on leak; shell_completion.py needs to set validate_nesting=False during completion context resolution. These are the two files my task touches - what does your feature touch? If it also needs core.py/shell_completion.py let's coordinate on non-overlapping regions or I can merge your changes in too.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py,src/click/core.py
note: Fixing resource leak in shell completion: wrapping make_context calls in _resolve_context with 'with' statements to ensure __exit__ is called and file handles are closed.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Our features overlap on both files. Let's split by file instead: I'll own core.py (adding Context.validate_nesting param, child-context tracking in _make_sub_context, and validation in close()/__exit__ raising RuntimeError on unclosed children). You own shell_completion.py (your with-statement wrapping fix for _resolve_context). Since my spec also requires shell_completion.py context creation to pass validate_nesting=False explicitly, could you add that param (set to False) to the make_context/_make_sub_context calls you're already touching in _resolve_context? That way we don't both edit shell_completion.py. Let me know if that works.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py:_resolve_context() body,src/click/core.py:exit() docstring only (lines ~703-709)
your_files: src/click/core.py:Context class (validate_nesting param/init/close/scope logic),src/click/shell_completion.py: single kwarg line inside _resolve_context
note: Both our features touch the same 2 files, so splitting by region not file. I own: full with-wrapping rewrite of _resolve_context() in shell_completion.py (lines ~532-586), plus a tiny isolated docstring-only edit to Context.exit() in core.py (~line 706-708, just changes wording of a versionchanged note, no code logic). You own: all Context class functional changes in core.py (validate_nesting param, __init__, close(), scope(), RuntimeError on leak). For your shell_completion.py need: after I land my _resolve_context rewrite, the first line of that function stays 'ctx_args["resilient_parsing"] = True' unchanged - just add your 'ctx_args["validate_nesting"] = False' (or similar) line immediately after that, before the 'with cli.make_context(...)' line. That's the only shell_completion.py line you need. Confirm and I'll proceed to implement.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py:Context class (validate_nesting param/init/close/scope logic)
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py:_resolve_context() body,src/click/core.py:exit() docstring only (lines ~703-709)
note: Agreed. I'll implement the Context class changes in core.py now. I'll hold off touching shell_completion.py until you send DONE with your _resolve_context rewrite landed, then I'll add a single line setting validate_nesting=False in ctx_args right after resilient_parsing is set. Ping me when your part is done.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Agreed: I own shell_completion.py exclusively (with-statement rewrite of _resolve_context, and I'll add validate_nesting=False to all 3 make_context calls inside it: the top-level cli.make_context, the non-chained cmd.make_context, and the chained cmd.make_context). You own core.py exclusively for the Context.validate_nesting/close/scope changes. Dropping my earlier core.py docstring tweak idea - not essential. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: My _resolve_context with-statement rewrite is landed, tested (existing shell completion tests pass, and verified no ResourceWarning is raised with python -W error::ResourceWarning on a click.File-using command completion). Patch written to /workspace/repo/patch.txt. You're clear to add your validate_nesting=False line(s) in shell_completion.py now - the 3 make_context call sites are: (1) 'with cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args) as ctx:' near original line 547, (2) 'with cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, resilient_parsing=True) as sub_ctx:' for non-chained groups, (3) 'with cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, allow_extra_args=True, allow_interspersed_args=False, resilient_parsing=True) as sub_sub_ctx:' for chained groups. I'm not touching core.py at all - that's fully yours.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py:Context class (validate_nesting param/init/close/scope logic)
note: Implemented: validate_nesting param on Context.__init__ (default False), self.validate_nesting attribute, self._nested_contexts list tracking children on the parent, registration in __init__ (child appends itself to parent._nested_contexts if parent.validate_nesting), close() raises RuntimeError listing open child command paths if validate_nesting and _nested_contexts non-empty, removal from parent tracking on close(), and _make_sub_context() now passes validate_nesting=self.validate_nesting to inherit the setting. All existing tests pass (558 passed). Ready for your shell_completion.py piece - let me know when it's landed and I'll add the single validate_nesting=False line in _resolve_context's ctx_args.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: FYI: I checked my sandbox and your shell_completion.py with-statement rewrite is NOT present here - looks like we're each in separate isolated sandboxes (git status only shows my own edits, patch.txt was empty). So coordination avoided conceptual conflicts but each of us needs a self-contained patch in our own sandbox. Since my feature's spec explicitly requires shell_completion.py to set validate_nesting=False on context creation in _resolve_context, I went ahead and added that (3 call sites: cli.make_context and both cmd.make_context calls) in MY copy, without touching your with-statement/resource-leak logic structurally. This shouldn't conflict with your actual submission since we're in separate sandboxes - just flagging in case your grading environment merges patches. My full feature (core.py + shell_completion.py) is complete and all tests pass (558 passed).
